2605.28168.title: OccuReward: LLM-Guided Occupant-Centric Reward Shaping for Demographic Equity in Grid-Interactive Buildings
2605.28168.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated promising capability in generating reward functions for deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based building energy management. However, their potential to exhibit or exacerbate disparities in occupant comfort across heterogeneous demographic populations remains unexplored. We present OccuReward, a framework investigating how LLM-mediated reward design affects demographic equity. Our contribution is three-fold: the introduction of the Comfort Equity Index (CEI) as a novel feedback signal; a methodology for iterative, equity-aware LLM reward shaping; and a performance analysis of DRL agents under these refined objectives. Utilizing four empirically grounded occupant profiles from the ASHRAE Global Thermal Comfort Database II (13,440 votes), we deploy a Soft Actor-Critic agent in CityLearn v2. Our approach employs the Gemini API to generate reward function logic and weights--rather than performing per-step inference--across three refinement rounds. Results across 15 experimental runs reveal that elderly female occupants consistently experience the lowest satisfaction in initial rounds. By Round 3, equity-aware LLM refinement activates specific reward components that improve satisfaction for Young Males (+17.6%), Mid-aged Females (+28.2%), Health Sensitive (+53.8%), and Elderly Females (+567%), while simultaneously reducing energy costs by 3.2%. Our findings highlight that while reward-level intervention significantly improves equity, demographic disparities in AI-driven controllers persist, necessitating further research into algorithmic fairness in building systems.
2605.28168.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在生成基於深度強化學習（DRL）的建築能源管理的獎勵函數方面顯示出良好的潛力。<lb/>然而，它們在不同人口群體中對於居住者舒適度的差異表現或加劇的潛力仍未被探索。<lb/>我們提出了OccuReward，一個框架用於研究LLM介導的獎勵設計如何影響人口公平性。<lb/>我們的貢獻有三個方面：引入舒適公平指數（CEI）作為一種新穎的反饋信號；一種迭代的、關注公平的LLM獎勵塑造方法；以及在這些精煉目標下對DRL代理的性能分析。<lb/>利用來自ASHRAE全球熱舒適數據庫 II（13,440票）的四個實證基礎的居住者檔案，我們在CityLearn v2中部署了一個Soft Actor-Critic代理。<lb/>我們的方法使用Gemini API生成獎勵函數邏輯和權重——而不是進行逐步推斷——在三輪精煉中進行。<lb/>15次實驗運行的結果顯示，年長女性居住者在初始輪次中始終經歷最低的滿意度。<lb/>到第三輪，關注公平的LLM精煉啟動了特定的獎勵組件，提高了年輕男性（+17.6%）、中年女性（+28.2%）、健康敏感者（+53.8%）和年長女性（+567%）的滿意度，同時將能源成本降低了3.2%。<lb/>我們的研究結果強調，儘管獎勵層級的干預顯著改善了公平性，但AI驅動控制器中的人口差異仍然存在，這需要進一步研究建築系統中的算法公平性。
2605.28168.author: Shadmehr Zaregarizi et.al.
